questions.text: According to the Quran (2:183), what is stated as the purpose of fasting in Ramadan?
Why does Ramadan start on a different date each year in the Gregorian calendar?
options: Scholars choose a new date each year
The Islamic calendar is lunar, roughly 11 days shorter yearly
It shifts due to leap years

questions.text: Which verse in the Quran states that fasting was also prescribed for previous nations?
Which companion of the Prophet was known as 'As-Siddiq' (The Truthful)?
If a fasting person eats or drinks by genuine forgetfulness, what is the ruling?
options: The fast is broken and must be made up
The fast is broken and requires kaffarah
The person must restart the fast immediately
The fast is broken but no makeup is needed

questions.text: What is the 'Fidyah' in the context of Ramadan?
options: A penalty for intentionally breaking the fast
Feeding a poor person for each missed fast day
An extra day of fasting after Ramadan

questions.text: Why is the Islamic calendar called 'Hijri' rather than 'Muhammadi'?
options: It was named by the Prophet himself
It commemorates the Hijrah, chosen by Umar as the starting point
It refers to the word for 'moon' in Arabic
It was named after the city of Hijr
It was decided by consensus of all companions

questions.text: How many gates does Paradise (Jannah) have according to Islamic tradition?
What is the 'Kaffarah' for intentionally breaking a Ramadan fast without valid reason?
options: Fasting 60 consecutive days or feeding 60 poor people
Paying a fine to the mosque
Making up only that one day

questions.text: The Quran mentions that Ramadan is the month in which what was specifically revealed?
options: The Quran as guidance for humanity
